leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: REWORKED per the rework note — merged main ([merge-update] commit takes main's side, dropping the prose-grep design) and re-implemented the gate on top (commit 1c6fdb9):  - **pr-review-report** (typed input, no prose-grep): `count_unresolved_page`/`unresolved_threads` read `reviewThreads { isResolved }` via GraphQL with pagination; new `OPEN_THREADS` bucket via `apply_threads_gate` — only otherwise-presentable buckets (APPROVED/AIVET/STALE_VET/UNREVIEWED) are gated, unknown thread state on a gated bucket surfaces as FETCH_ERROR (never silently presentable); `--queue` excludes thread-dirty PRs fail-closed (`threads_presentable`), matching the existing mergeable check. - **campaign-prompt**: producer half folded into step 2z FLEET GREENNESS (THREAD RESOLUTION: fix → push `[3e-attempt]` (one per PR ever) → reply → `resolveReviewThread`); step 7 allow-list extended to thread replies/resolution on own PRs only, after the addressing commit. - **review-prompt**: OPEN-THREADS GATE sits beside the QA GATE; prose-grepping "Actionable comments posted" is explicitly forbidden; vetter records no verdict on thread-dirty PRs and reports them as "open-threads (skipped)".  QA evidence (PR body is pre-restructure; producer cannot edit bodies, so it lives here):  ``` ## QA - Discriminating tests: open_threads_tests T1-T9 (count_mixed_resolved_unresolved, count_empty_nodes_is_zero, count_propagates_next_cursor, count_malformed_is_none, gated_set_is_presentable_buckets_only, gate_routes_gated_buckets, gate_ignores_non_gated_buckets, queue_presentability_fail_closed, open_threads_key) — the tested functions do not exist on base, so each fails on base by construction (compile error); full suite 57/57 green on the branch. - Mutations applied: apply_threads_gate 'Some(_) => OpenThreads' → 'Some(_) => b' → killed by gate_routes_gated_buckets; count_unresolved_page '!isResolved' → 'isResolved' → killed by count_mixed_resolved_unresolved (+1); threads_presentable 'threads == Some(0)' → 'unwrap_or(0) == 0' → killed by queue_presentability_fail_closed; threads_gated minus Unreviewed → killed by gated_set_is_presentable_buckets_only. All restored; suite re-run green. - Oracle: GitHub GraphQL reviewThreads schema (isResolved/pageInfo) — fixtures hand-built from the documented response shape, independent of the implementation. - Category check: issue #1 asks CodeRabbit findings to block human review; covered via unresolved-thread state (supersedes prose-grep per rework note) across report buckets, queue filter, producer duty, and vetter gate. Caveat: the report-loop call-site wiring (apply_threads_gate around bucket_of) and the live GraphQL call are covered by the nix build + review, not unit tests — the pure logic is. ```  CI gate (`nix build .#pr-review-report`) passes locally on the pushed tree.

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: reworked per the rework note — the enumeration is now genuinely single-source.  - Removed the hand-synced copies the PR had added: `FORK_VAULT_COUNT`, the per-vault `WT_*` imports, and `testForkVaultEnumeration`'s parallel address + label tables (which also duplicated the existing `allForkVaults()` loop at double fork cost). - `allForkVaults()` is now literally the single source: the `WT_*` constants had no consumer other than the registry itself, so their addresses were inlined into `allForkVaults()`. Count is `.length`, labels come from `vm.toString` in the existing `testAllForkVaults` loop (unchanged). - Added `test/lib/LibFork.t.sol` with `testAllForkVaultsEnumeration`: asserts the registry is non-empty with no zero and no duplicate addresses. This is a real single-source lock without hand-copies, and it runs on every CI pass (no fork needed). - Verified locally: full `forge build` clean, new enumeration test passes. The fork loop itself rides on fork CI as before. - Per the note, #139's remaining legs (hand-pinned expected rates, fork-block pin policy) are left for issue refinement / a design ruling, not this mechanical test PR.  Branch also carries the previously-unpushed merge of origin/main (conflict resolution), now pushed.

leaks.reason: 🤖 ai:producer Producer note: rework NOT executed as written — the note's premise conflicts with the rainix reusable contract. The rainix-sol reusable's test job maps secret RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK → job env FLARE_RPC_URL (rainix .github/workflows/rainix-sol-test.yaml: `FLARE_RPC_URL: ${{ secrets.RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK || vars.RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK }}`), and this repo's `secrets: inherit` already passes the secret through — so the branch's current `vm.envString("FLARE_RPC_URL")` is the name the CI job env actually exposes, and this PR's checks are green with it. Reading `RPC_URL_FLARE_FORK` directly (the note's ask) would revert in CI because no env var of that name exists inside the job. Issue #67's intent (fork tests read the org fork-RPC secret, no public-RPC fallback) is satisfied through the mapping. Awaiting your ruling on env-name convention before any further change here.
